subject: Geology
title: Critical Minerals for Green Energy
description: A quick win: understand Critical Minerals for Green Energy.

hook.text: Solar panels need silicon. Batteries need lithium and cobalt. The green energy shift demands a mining boom.

buildup.text: Transitioning to clean energy requires large amounts of specific minerals. Demand is surging for lithium and cobalt.

discovery.text: Most cobalt comes from the Congo, often mined under poor conditions. Supply chains carry ethical and geologic risks.

twist.text: New battery chemistries aim to reduce reliance on scarce minerals. Sodium-ion and solid-state designs are emerging.

climax.text: Green energy is not free of geologic cost. Choosing sustainability means managing mineral supply responsibly.

punchline.text: Clean energy still starts with mining.

quiz.question: Why is cobalt supply a concern for green energy?
options: Cobalt is too common to be valuable
Most cobalt comes from one country with supply and ethical risks
Cobalt is only used in fossil fuel plants
